description: Build the comparison report generator (src/lib/baseline-comparison.ts) and the runnable comparison script (scripts/run-comparison.ts). The comparison module computes: (1) primary metric - count of distinct risk/decision categories surfaced by baseline vs union across all 4 debate agents' consensus output, (2) secondary metric - token/cost overhead for full 4-agent debate vs single baseline call, (3) optional coverage check - whether the baseline clusters on 1-2 lenses vs touching all 4. The script ties it together by running both arms (or accepting pre-computed results) and producing a JSON+markdown report.
steps: Create src/lib/baseline-comparison.ts. Define interfaces: ComparisonInput (baselineResult: BaselineResult from baseline-runner, debateConsensus: ConsensusOutput from domain.ts, debateTokenUsage: { inputTokens, outputTokens }, baselineTokenUsage: { inputTokens, outputTokens }), ComparisonReport (categories object with baseline-only, debate-only, both arrays; tokenCost object with baseline and debate totals; coverageAnalysis with per-discipline boolean flags for baseline).

Implement category extraction: from ProposalOutput, extract risk categories (risk.description mapped to a normalized category string) and decision categories (from artifactSuggestions where type is 'decision' or 'recommendation', using title as category). From ConsensusOutput, extract from identifiedRisks and recommendedDecisions similarly.

Implement the comparison function generateComparisonReport(input: ComparisonInput): ComparisonReport. It should: (a) extract categories from baseline ProposalOutput, (b) extract categories from debate ConsensusOutput (union of identifiedRisks + recommendedDecisions), (c) compute overlap (categories in both), baseline-only, and debate-only sets, (d) compute token costs for both arms, (e) perform coverage check - classify each baseline category into which discipline lens it addresses (architecture/security/performance/product) using keyword heuristics.

Implement formatComparisonMarkdown(report: ComparisonReport): string that produces a readable markdown report with sections: Summary, Category Comparison (with counts and lists), Token/Cost Analysis, Coverage Analysis, and Raw Data (JSON).

Create scripts/ directory. Create scripts/run-comparison.ts - a standalone script that: (a) accepts a GitHub repo URL and problem description as arguments or from a config object, (b) runs the baseline via runBaseline, (c) NOTE: running the full debate is complex (requires session/DB); instead the script should accept a pre-computed ConsensusOutput JSON file path as input OR run only the baseline and output its results for manual comparison, (d) generates and prints the comparison report. Add a package.json script entry for running it with tsx/ts-node.

Create src/lib/baseline-comparison.test.ts with unit tests using synthetic data. Test: (1) category extraction correctly identifies risks and decisions from ProposalOutput and ConsensusOutput, (2) overlap computation is correct (items in both, only-baseline, only-debate), (3) token cost comparison correctly reports both arms, (4) coverage analysis correctly classifies categories into discipline lenses, (5) markdown formatter produces valid output with all sections.
acceptance_criteria: Comparison report shows category overlap/difference computed deterministically (not eyeballed)
Token/cost numbers for both arms are in the same report
Coverage analysis classifies baseline categories by discipline lens
scripts/run-comparison.ts exists and is runnable (produces output when given mock/real inputs)
Unit tests pass: npx vitest run src/lib/baseline-comparison.test.ts
verification: Run npx vitest run src/lib/baseline-comparison.test.ts and confirm all tests pass
Run npx tsc --noEmit and confirm no type errors
findings: tsx added as devDependency for running the comparison script. fetchRepoTree returns RepoTree | GithubError union type requiring error handling. The 'profil' performance keyword was too broad (matched 'profile' in unrelated contexts) - changed to 'profiling' for specificity. Pre-existing type errors confirmed in output-validator.test.ts, prompt-builder.test.ts, ui-logic.test.ts (not from this change).
